meta.title: Getting Started - Infrastructure & Operations
meta.excerpt: Complete guide to infrastructure setup, environment configuration, database operations, and deployment to Vercel.

seo.metaDescription: Complete infrastructure setup guide covering environment variables, PostgreSQL configuration, Vercel deployment, and operational workflows.

blocks.content: The application infrastructure consists of two main components: Next.js frontend (deployed on Vercel) and Strapi CMS (self-hosted or cloud). Both connect to a PostgreSQL database.

features.title: Next.js on Vercel
features.description: Static site generation with ISR, edge functions, and automatic deployments from Git.

features.title: Strapi CMS
features.description: Self-hosted on DigitalOcean/AWS or Strapi Cloud with PostgreSQL database.

features.title: PostgreSQL Database
features.description: Managed database service (Supabase, Railway, or self-hosted) with automated backups.

blocks.content: Proper environment configuration is critical for security and functionality. Here are the required environment variables:

blocks.title: .env.local (Next.js)
blocks.code:
# Strapi API
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:1337
STRAPI_API_TOKEN=your_api_token_here

# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/db

# Email (Resend)
RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxxxx
EMAIL_FROM=noreply@example.com

# Environment
NODE_ENV=development

blocks.content: Setting up PostgreSQL for local development:

items: Install PostgreSQL locally or use Docker

blocks.content: Deploy the Next.js application to Vercel with automated CI/CD from your Git repository. Configure production environment variables in the Vercel dashboard.
